tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 5 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text:
{"number":57057,"title":"[Mgmt Generator Bug] Collection emits IEnumerable boilerplate for non-pageable list operation","body":"## Bug Description

The generator emits standard `IEnumerable.GetEnumerator()` and `IAsyncEnumerable.GetAsyncEnumerator()` implementations that call `GetAll().GetEnumerator()`, but `GetAll()` returns `Response` (a single-response list operation) rather than a pageable collection, causing CS1061 errors.

## Impact

**3 errors** in SecurityInsights migration (#56944).

## How to Reproduce

1. Clone `azure-sdk-for-net` and `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Checkout branch `securityinsights-typespec-migration` in azure-sdk-for-net
3. Checkout branch `securityinsights-csharp-migration` in azure-rest-api-specs
4. Run code generation:
```bash
cd sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src
dotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=/specification/securityinsights/resource-manager/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/SecurityInsights
```
5. Build: `dotnet build`
6. Observe CS1061 errors in `SecurityInsightsSentinelOnboardingStateCollection.cs`

## Root Cause

The `SentinelOnboardingStates.list` operation returns a single non-paginated list (`SentinelOnboardingStatesList`), not a pageable collection. The generator still emits the standard collection `IEnumerable`/`IAsyncEnumerable` boilerplate that expects `GetAll()` to return an enumerable.

**Example:** `SecurityInsightsSentinelOnboardingStateCollection.cs`:
```csharp
// GetAll() returns Response, NOT a pageable
public virtual Response GetAll(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)

// But the generator emits:
IEnumerator IEnumerable<...>.GetEnumerator()
{
    return GetAll().GetEnumerator();  // BUG: Response has no GetEnumerator()
}

IAsyncEnumerator<...> IAsyncEnumerable<...>.GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
{
    return GetAllAsync(...).GetAsyncEnumerator(cancellationToken);  // BUG: Task> has no GetAsyncEnumerator()
}
```

## Expected Behavior

For non-pageable list operations, the generator should either:
- Not emit the `IEnumerable`/`IAsyncEnumerable` interface implementations, or
- Unwrap the response value to get the list and iterate over it (e.g., `GetAll().Value.Value.GetEnumerator()`)

## Affected Files

- `SecurityInsightsSentinelOnboardingStateCollection.cs` (lines 595, 600, 606)

{"total_count":2,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4069395776,"number":57057,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Mgmt Generator Bug] Collection emits IEnumerable boilerplate for non-pageable list operation","body":"## Bug Description

The generator emits standard `IEnumerable<T>.GetEnumerator()` and `IAsyncEnumerable<T>.GetAsyncEnumerator()` implementations that call `GetAll().GetEnumerator()`, but `GetAll()` returns `Response<TList>` (a single-response list operation) rather than a pageable collection, causing CS1061 errors.

## Impact

**3 errors** in SecurityInsights migration (#56944).

## How to Reproduce

1. Clone `azure-sdk-for-net` and `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Checkout branch `securityinsights-typespec-migration` in azure-sdk-for-net
3. Checkout branch `securityinsights-csharp-migration` in azure-rest-api-specs
4. Run code generation:
```bash
cd sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src
dotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=<path-to-azure-rest-api-specs>/specification/securityinsights/resource-manager/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/SecurityInsights
```
5. Build: `dotnet build`
6. Observe CS1061 errors in `SecurityInsightsSentinelOnboardingStateCollection.cs`

## Root Cause

The `SentinelOnboardingStates.list` operation returns a single non-paginated list (`SentinelOnboardingStatesList`), not a pageable collection. The generator still emits the standard collection `IEnumerable`/`IAsyncEnumerable` boilerplate that expects `GetAll()` to return an enumerable.

**Example:** `SecurityInsightsSentinelOnboardingStateCollection.cs`:
```csharp
// GetAll() returns Response<SentinelOnboardingStatesList>, NOT a pageable
public virtual Response<SentinelOnboardingStatesList> GetAll(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)

// But the generator emits:
IEnumerator<SecurityInsightsSentinelOnboardingStateResource> IEnumerable<...>.GetEnumerator()
{
    return GetAll().GetEnumerator();  // BUG: Response<T> has no GetEnumerator()
}

IAsyncEnumerator<...> IAsyncEnumerable<...>.GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
{
    return GetAllAsync(...).GetAsyncEnumerator(cancellationToken);  // BUG: Task<Response<T>> has no GetAsyncEnumerator()
}
```

## Expected Behavior

For non-pageable list operations, the generator should either:
- Not emit the `IEnumerable`/`IAsyncEnumerable` interface implementations, or
- Unwrap the response value to get the list and iterate over it (e.g., `GetAll().Value.Value.GetEnumerator()`)

## Affected Files

- `SecurityInsightsSentinelOnboardingStateCollection.cs` (lines 595, 600, 606)

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** The Azure Management Generator emits `IEnumerable<T>`/`IAsyncEnumerable<T>` interface implementations that unconditionally call `GetAll().GetEnumerator()`, but when a list operation is non-pageable and returns `Response<TList>` (a complex wrapper type rather than a list), `GetAll()` returns `Response<T>` — which has no `GetEnumerator()` method — causing CS1061 build errors. First surfaced in SecurityInsights migration (#56944).

<details>
<summary>🔍 Root Cause Analysis</summary>

The issue is in **`ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs`** in `/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/`.

**The guard method `ShouldSkipIEnumerableImplementation()`** (lines ~215–218) currently only skips the boilerplate if `_getAllSyncMethodProvider` is `null` or if `GetAll()` has required (non-default) parameters:

```csharp
private bool ShouldSkipIEnumerableImplementation()
{
    return _getAllSyncMethodProvider is null 
        || _getAllSyncMethodProvider.Signature.Parameters.Any(p => p.DefaultValue is null);
}
```

**The `BuildNonPagingGetAllMethod()`** (lines ~419–430) dispatches to:
- `ArrayResponseOperationMethodProvider` — when the response body type is a list (can call `.GetEnumerator()` ✅)
- `ResourceOperationMethodProvider` — when the response body is a complex wrapper type like `SentinelOnboardingStatesList` (returns `Response<T>` — calling `.GetEnumerator()` on this is ❌ CS1061)

The `ShouldSkipIEnumerableImplementation()` check does **not** distinguish between these two cases, so `IEnumerable`/`IAsyncEnumerable` boilerplate gets emitted even when `GetAll()` returns `Response<T>`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🛠️ Suggested Fix</summary>

In `ShouldSkipIEnumerableImplementation()`, add a check for whether `_getAllSyncMethodProvider` is a `ResourceOperationMethodProvider` (non-pageable, non-array result). If so, skip the boilerplate. Example approach:

```csharp
private bool ShouldSkipIEnumerableImplementation()
{
    return _getAllSyncMethodProvider is null
        || _getAllSyncMethodProvider.Signature.Parameters.Any(p => p.DefaultValue is null)
        || _getAllSyncMethodProvider is ResourceOperationMethodProvider; // ← add this
}
```

Alternatively, check the return type of `_getAllSyncMethodProvider`: if it's not a pageable type (i.e., not `Pageable<T>` or `AsyncPageable<T>`), skip boilerplate generation.

**Two valid outcomes per the issue description:**
1. **Skip the boilerplate entirely** for non-pageable collections — clean and avoids false interface contracts.
2. **Unwrap the response** — emit `GetAll().Value.Value.GetEnumerator()` — but this is brittle and depends on the nested type structure.

Option 1 is recommended.

</details>

<details>
<summary>📁 Affected Files</summary>

- **Bug location (generator):** `/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs`
  - `ShouldSkipIEnumerableImplementation()` (~line 215)
  - `BuildNonPagingGetAllMethod()` (~line 419)
- **Affected generated file (SecurityInsights):** `sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src/Generated/SecurityInsightsSentinelOnboardingStateCollection.cs` (lines 595, 600, 606)

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔬 Reproduction Steps</summary>

1. Clone `azure-sdk-for-net` and `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Checkout `securityinsights-typespec-migration` in azure-sdk-for-net
3. Checkout `securityinsights-csharp-migration` in azure-rest-api-specs
4. Run:
   ```bash
   cd sdk/securityinsights/Azure.ResourceManager.SecurityInsights/src
   dotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=<path>/specification/securityinsights/resource-manager/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/SecurityInsights
   dotnet build
   ```
5. Observe CS1061 in `SecurityInsightsSentinelOnboardingStateCollection.cs`

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ Sub-tasks Checklist</summary>

- [ ] Update `ShouldSkipIEnumerableImplementation()` in `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs` to return `true` when `_getAllSyncMethodProvider` is a `ResourceOperationMethodProvider` (non-pageable, non-array response)
- [ ] Add/update a unit test or test TypeSpec spec that exercises a non-pageable list operation (single-response list) to ensure the boilerplate is not emitted
- [ ] Regenerate test projects via `/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` to validate no regressions
- [ ] Verify SecurityInsights migration (#56944) builds successfully after the fix

</details>

<details>
<summary>📚 Relevant Resources</summary>

- Generator source: [`ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs)
- Parent migration issue: #56944
- Management Generator readme: [`/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt)

</details>
